LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED IS ARGUED. AFTER ARGUMENTS, THE COURT GRANTS A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. PARTIES STATED THEIR AGREEMENT ON THE LANGUAGE OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION AS STATED ON THE RECORD. (AS FULLY REPORTED ON THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) PARTIES TO JOINTLY SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT BOTH SIDES HAVE AGREED ON IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. IF PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO AGREE, BOTH SIDES CAN SUBMIT THEIR OWN PROPOSED ORDERS THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING DURING THE HEARING. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-26-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued (Hearing Is Specially Set For 1:30 P.M.)
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ernst Halperin, Assistant District Attorney 415-551-9545 and Evan Ackiron, Managing Attorney 415-551-9560 of the Office of the District Atorney for Plaintiff the People of the State of California.
Collie James IV, Esq. 949-399-7199 of Morgan Lewis for Defendant Home Advisor and ANGI Homeservices.
Allison Lowrie, Client Marketing Officer 303-963-7755 Expert for Defendant Home Advisor.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares his tentative ruling in favor of the people on the visual disclaimer and in favor of Homeadvisor on the audio disclaimer. Plaintiff argues, shows video (not reported). Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued.

After arguments, The Court Grants a Preliminary Injunction. Parties stated their agreement on the language of the Preliminary Injunction as stated on the record. (as fully reported on the reporter's transcript)

Parties to jointly submit a proposed order that both sides have agreed on in compliance with CRC 3.1312. If parties are unable to agree, both sides can submit their own proposed orders that is verbatim with the Court's ruling during the hearing.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ernst Halperin, Assistant District Attorney 415-551-9545 for Plaintiff the People of the State of California.
Collie James IV, Esq. 949-399-7199 of Morgan Lewis for Defendant Home Advisor and ANGI Homeservices.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court inquires re: the status of the preliminary injunction. Plaintiff reports Impasse on wording and language, submits list and discs to the Court. Both sides argue. The Court goes through the issues.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes)

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued.

Continued to 5/10/18 at 1:30 pm. Parties to jointly submit a list of each issue in dispute and materials to be reviewed no later than 5 pm on Wednesday 5/9/18 to Dept. 302.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Apr-25-2018 Continued To May-08-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy District Attorneys, Ernst A. Halperin and Evan H. Ackiron of the San Francisco District Attorney's Office (415-551-9545) appear as counsels for plaintiff/injunction applicant, The People of the State of California

Attorney, Collie F. James, IV of the law offices of Morgan Lewis & Bockius LLP (714-830-0600) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Homeadvisor, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is CONTINUED to May 8, 2018 at 9:00 AM in Department 302/Law and motion. notice waived.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Continued To Apr-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is CONTINUED to April 25, 2018 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 per agreement of the parties and order of court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued.
Off Calendar Apr-18-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ernst Halperin, Esq., Asst. District Attorney, T:415.551.9545, for plaintiff;
Collie F. James, IV, Esq. of Morgan Lewis, T:949.399.7199, for defendant.

RULING: The Court heard continued discussion/arguments from both parties. Judge Kahn disclosed that he has heard several Homeadvisor's Ads recently and none of them made reference to screening or background checks. After hearing,

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued is CONTINUED to APRIL 23, 2018 at 9:00am. Parties are to continue working on the language of the disclaimer, which should include the issue of conspicuousness/font.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.

Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued
Off Calendar Apr-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ernst Halperin, Esq., Asst. District Attorney, T:415.551.9545, for plaintiff/moving party;
Collie R. James, IV, Esq., of Morgan Lewis, T:949.399.7199, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: Hearing held. After hearing, the Court continues Order To Show Cause Why Preliminary Injunction Should Not Be Issued to April 18, 2018 to be heard at 9:00 am. Parties are to meet and confer about the form of order. If both sides wish to continue the hearing they must send an email to the Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Christine Jordan, CSR#12262, email: cjordan@sftc.org.
